Name: Teacher
Text: Hello, Noah. I’ve been waiting for you.

Name: Noah
Text: Oh, hi. I heard you have something to ask me for?

Name: Teacher
Text: Yes... One of my students was reported for cheating.

Name: Teacher
Text: There’s a witness, so I asked for details, but the student insists they didn’t do it.

Name: Teacher
Text: Yes. Usually, when there’s a witness, students admit to it quickly, but something feels off.

Name: Teacher
Text: I hope you can find out the truth or identify the real culprit.

Name: Noah
Text: Got it. Can I speak to the students first?

Name: Student2
Text: Stop lying! Teacher, I saw it!

Name: Teacher
Text: Kids, quiet down. Don’t yell in front of our guest.

Name: Teacher
Text: Now, you first, explain what happened.

Name: Student1
Text: I was just taking the test, that’s all. I didn’t do anything!

Name: Student2
Text: No! I saw it! They were using their phone during class!

Name: Noah
Text: Hmm.... If it was during the test, your head would have been down the whole time.

Name: Noah
Text: How could you have seen that?

Name: Student2
Text: ...I just saw it when I looked up! I’m sure of it!

Name: Noah
Text: I see. Teacher, may I check the classroom for a moment?
